result.stdout:
Description:
  Interact with a Curiosity Workspace

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  upload-front-end                Update the front-end on the workspace with the content of the folder passed as input
  test                            Test the connection to the workspace
  store-token                     Store the token for a given workspace locally. You can re-use the stored token by passing --token auto on other commands
  serve                           Serve locally a front-end project built with the Mosaik.FrontEnd nuget package
  inspect                         Inspect a folder for supported files, and return a summary.
  ingest-pst                      Upload emails extracted from a PST file
  upload-folder                   Upload a folder to the workspace
  monitor                         Monitor and sync a folder to the workspace
  git-sync                        Sync a git tracker from the workspace locally
  wait-for                        Wait for a workspace to be ready
  upload-folder-with-permissions  Upload a network folder to the workspace and sync Active Directory access permissions
  monitor-with-permissions        Monitor and sync a network folder to the workspace, including Active Directory access permissions
  export-workspace-definitions    Export custom code from the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints
  import-workspace-definitions    Import custom code into the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints

commands.payload:
Description:
  Interact with a Curiosity Workspace

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information
  --version       Show version information

Commands:
  upload-front-end                Update the front-end on the workspace with the content of the folder passed as input
  test                            Test the connection to the workspace
  store-token                     Store the token for a given workspace locally. You can re-use the stored token by passing --token auto on other commands
  serve                           Serve locally a front-end project built with the Mosaik.FrontEnd nuget package
  inspect                         Inspect a folder for supported files, and return a summary.
  ingest-pst                      Upload emails extracted from a PST file
  upload-folder                   Upload a folder to the workspace
  monitor                         Monitor and sync a folder to the workspace
  git-sync                        Sync a git tracker from the workspace locally
  wait-for                        Wait for a workspace to be ready
  upload-folder-with-permissions  Upload a network folder to the workspace and sync Active Directory access permissions
  monitor-with-permissions        Monitor and sync a network folder to the workspace, including Active Directory access permissions
  export-workspace-definitions    Export custom code from the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints
  import-workspace-definitions    Import custom code into the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints

result.stdout:
Description:
  Export custom code from the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI export-workspace-definitions [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)  URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)    Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  --export-path <export-path>       Path to store the exported zip file (or output directory when unzip = true).
  -u, --unzip                       If true, export to a directory by downloading the zip and extracting it.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Sync a git tracker from the workspace locally

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI git-sync [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)  URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)    Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)      Folder to local git repository
  -i, --ignore-certificate-errors   Ignore certificate errors
  -v, --verbose                     Verbose mode
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Import custom code into the workspace: custom tasks, custom indexes, custom endpoints

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI import-workspace-definitions [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)  URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)    Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  --import-path <import-path>       Path to a .zip file containing your workspace definitions (or directory when zip = true)
  -z, --zip                         If true, zip the import directory before uploading it.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload emails extracted from a PST file

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI ingest-pst [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                          URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                            Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                              PST file to analyze and upload emails from to the workspace
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                   Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --source <source>                                         Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the file name of the specified file.
  --upload-to <Custom|Group|Public|UserPrivate|UserPublic>  Where to store the file. Possible values are: Public, Group, UserPublic or UserPrivate [default: Public]
  --target-uid <target-uid>                                 If you pass Group or UserPublic/UserPrivate to the --upload-to parameter, you need to also specify the UID of the Group/User
  --ignore-folders <ignore-folders>                         A semicolon (';') delimited list of case-sensitive folder names to skip (including any sub folders) - if not specified then a default set will be used but this may not cover all folders that should be bypassed if the folder names are translated into different languages
  --timeout <timeout>                                       Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  -?, -h, --help                                            Show help and usage information

  --target-uid <target-uid>                                 If you pass Group or UserPublic/UserPrivate to the --upload-to parameter, you need to also specify the UID of the Group/User
  --ignore-folders <ignore-folders>                         A semicolon (';') delimited list of case-sensitive folder names to skip (including any sub folders) - if not specified then a default set will be used but this may not cover all folders that should be bypassed if the folder names are translated into different languages
  --timeout <timeout>                                       Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]

result.stdout:
Description:
  Monitor and sync a folder to the workspace

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI monitor [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                          URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                            Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                              Folder to monitor and sync to the workspace
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                   Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --fetch-server-state (REQUIRED)                           Fetch the already synchronized files before starting a sync. This is required to make sure that locally deleted files which are also deleted from the workspace. Requires syncFileUrl
  --restore-access-time                                     Restore last access date after reading file (only recomended for hot+cold storage network drives)
  --source <source>                                         Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the name of the directory of the given Path.
  --upload-to <Custom|Group|Public|UserPrivate|UserPublic>  Where to store the file. Possible values are: Public, Group, UserPublic or UserPrivate [default: Public]
  --target-uid <target-uid>                                 If you pass Group or UserPublic/UserPrivate to the --upload-to parameter, you need to also specify the UID of the Group/User
  --extensions <extensions>                                 Extensions to upload, split by ';' - example: --extensions doc;docx;pdf;
  --root-path <root-path>                                   Name of the root Path to be replaced by the root-folder-name in the workspace.
  --root-folder-name <root-folder-name>                     Name of the root folder created in the workspace. If none is given, the folders are created under the workspace root folder.
  --timeout <timeout>                                       Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  --sync-file-url                                           Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly.
  --in-place                                                ONLY Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly. The server needs direct access to the files to make them searchable. No files are copied.
  -?, -h, --help                                            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Monitor and sync a network folder to the workspace, including Active Directory access permissions

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI monitor-with-permissions [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                        URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                          Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -c, --permissions-cache <permissions-cache> (REQUIRED)  Path to a .json file where to store the permissions cache
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                            Folder to monitor and sync to the workspace
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                 Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --fetch-server-state (REQUIRED)                         Fetch the already synchronized files before starting a sync. This is required to make sure that locally deleted files which are also deleted from the workspace. Requires syncFileUrl
  --restore-access-time                                   Restore last access date after reading file (only recomended for hot+cold storage network drives)
  --source <source>                                       Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the name of the directory of the given Path.
  --extensions <extensions>                               Extensions to upload, split by ';' - example: --extensions doc;docx;pdf;
  --root-path <root-path>                                 Name of the root Path to be replaced by the root-folder-name in the workspace.
  --root-folder-name <root-folder-name>                   Name of the root folder created in the workspace. If none is given, the folders are created under the workspace root folder.
  --timeout <timeout>                                     Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  --sync-file-url                                         Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly.
  --in-place                                              ONLY Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly. The server needs direct access to the files to make them searchable. No files are copied.
  -?, -h, --help                                          Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Serve locally a front-end project built with the Mosaik.FrontEnd nuget package

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI serve [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server>            URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -p, --path <path>                Path to output folder from the compiled front-end. Example .\MyProject\bin\Debug\netstandard2.0\h5\
  --name <name>                    Name of the app, to be shown for example on the navbar of the UI [default: Mosaik]
  --ignore-certificate-errors      Ignore certificate errors
  --port <port>                    Port to bind locally [default: 5000]
  --cert-file <cert-file>          Certificate file to bind, only required if binding to https
  --cert-password <cert-password>  Certificate file password, only required if binding to https and if certificate is password protected
  --listen-to-any                  Pass if you would like to list to connections from any IP, instead of only connections from localhost
  --enable-cors                    Enable CORS on local hosting
  --path-base <path-base>          Base path to listen, default to none
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Serve locally a front-end project built with the Mosaik.FrontEnd nuget package

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI serve [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server>            URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -p, --path <path>                Path to output folder from the compiled front-end. Example .\MyProject\bin\Debug\netstandard2.0\h5\
  --name <name>                    Name of the app, to be shown for example on the navbar of the UI [default: Mosaik]
  --ignore-certificate-errors      Ignore certificate errors
  --port <port>                    Port to bind locally [default: 5000]
  --cert-file <cert-file>          Certificate file to bind, only required if binding to https
  --cert-password <cert-password>  Certificate file password, only required if binding to https and if certificate is password protected
  --listen-to-any                  Pass if you would like to list to connections from any IP, instead of only connections from localhost
  --enable-cors                    Enable CORS on local hosting
  --path-base <path-base>          Base path to listen, default to none
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload a folder to the workspace

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI upload-folder [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                          URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                            Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                              Folder to upload to the workspace
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                   Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --fetch-server-state (REQUIRED)                           Fetch the already synchronized files before starting a sync. This is required to make sure that files that are deleted locally are also deleted from the workspace.  Requires syncFileUrl
  --restore-access-time                                     Restore last access date after reading file (only recomended for hot+cold storage network drives)
  --source <source>                                         Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the name of the directory of the given Path.
  --upload-to <Custom|Group|Public|UserPrivate|UserPublic>  Where to store the file. Possible values are: Public, Group, UserPublic or UserPrivate [default: Public]
  --target-uid <target-uid>                                 If you pass Group or UserPublic/UserPrivate to the --upload-to parameter, you need to also specify the UID of the Group/User
  --extensions <extensions>                                 Extensions to upload, split by ';' - example: --extensions doc;docx;pdf;
  --root-path <root-path>                                   Name of the root Path to be replaced by the root-folder-name in the workspace.
  --root-folder-name <root-folder-name>                     Name of the root folder created in the workspace. If none is given, the folders are created under the workspace root folder.
  --timeout <timeout>                                       Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  --sync-file-url                                           Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly.
  --in-place                                                ONLY Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly. The server needs direct access to the files to make them searchable. No files are copied.
  -?, -h, --help                                            Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Upload a network folder to the workspace and sync Active Directory access permissions

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI upload-folder-with-permissions [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                        URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                          Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                            Folder to upload to the workspace
  -c, --permissions-cache <permissions-cache> (REQUIRED)  Path to a .json file where to store the permissions cache
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                 Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --fetch-server-state (REQUIRED)                         Fetch the already synchronized files before starting a sync. This is required to make sure that files that are deleted locally are also deleted from the workspace.  Requires syncFileUrl
  --restore-access-time                                   Restore last access date after reading file (only recomended for hot+cold storage network drives)
  --source <source>                                       Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the name of the directory of the given Path.
  --extensions <extensions>                               Extensions to upload, split by ';' - example: --extensions doc;docx;pdf;
  --root-path <root-path>                                 Name of the root Path to be replaced by the root-folder-name in the workspace.
  --root-folder-name <root-folder-name>                   Name of the root folder created in the workspace. If none is given, the folders are created under the workspace root folder.
  --timeout <timeout>                                     Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  --sync-file-url                                         Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly.
  --in-place                                              ONLY Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly. The server needs direct access to the files to make them searchable. No files are copied.
  -?, -h, --help                                          Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Upload a network folder to the workspace and sync Active Directory access permissions

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI upload-folder-with-permissions [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server> (REQUIRED)                        URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token> (REQUIRED)                          Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path> (REQUIRED)                            Folder to upload to the workspace
  -c, --permissions-cache <permissions-cache> (REQUIRED)  Path to a .json file where to store the permissions cache
  --bandwidth <bandwidth>                                 Maximum Bandwith in Mbps
  --fetch-server-state (REQUIRED)                         Fetch the already synchronized files before starting a sync. This is required to make sure that files that are deleted locally are also deleted from the workspace.  Requires syncFileUrl
  --restore-access-time                                   Restore last access date after reading file (only recomended for hot+cold storage network drives)
  --source <source>                                       Name of the source, to be added to each file entry. The default is the name of the directory of the given Path.
  --extensions <extensions>                               Extensions to upload, split by ';' - example: --extensions doc;docx;pdf;
  --root-path <root-path>                                 Name of the root Path to be replaced by the root-folder-name in the workspace.
  --root-folder-name <root-folder-name>                   Name of the root folder created in the workspace. If none is given, the folders are created under the workspace root folder.
  --timeout <timeout>                                     Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  --sync-file-url                                         Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly.
  --in-place                                              ONLY Sync the file url to the workspace. Uploaded files then can be opened directly. The server needs direct access to the files to make them searchable. No files are copied.
  -?, -h, --help                                          Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Update the front-end on the workspace with the content of the folder passed as input

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI upload-front-end [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server>            URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -t, --token <token>              Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path>                Path to output folder from the compiled front-end. Example .\MyProject\bin\Debug\netstandard2.0\h5\
  -a, --auto-patch                 If it should automatically patch instead of replacing all files
  -i, --ignore-certificate-errors  Ignore certificate errors
  --timeout <timeout>              Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:

Options:

  -t, --token <token>              Library Token, can be created on the workspace under Manage > Tokens > Library. The token can also be encrypted and stored locally with the store-token tool.
  -p, --path <path>                Path to output folder from the compiled front-end. Example .\MyProject\bin\Debug\netstandard2.0\h5\
  -a, --auto-patch                 If it should automatically patch instead of replacing all files
  -i, --ignore-certificate-errors  Ignore certificate errors
  --timeout <timeout>              Timeout for requests in seconds. (default: 90 secs) [default: 90]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Wait for a workspace to be ready

Usage:
  Curiosity.CLI wait-for [options]

Options:
  -s, --server <server>            URL to reach the workspace, such as 'https://my-workspace.curiosity.ai/'.
  -i, --ignore-certificate-errors  Ignore certificate errors
  --max-timeout <max-timeout>      Maximum time to wait in seconds. (default: 1800 secs) [default: 1800]
  -?, -h, --help                   Show help and usage information
